   181→	// specified values. Containers completed with success (exit code 0) are
   182→	// excluded from the requirement check. Possible values are:
   183→	//
   184→	// - In: the requirement is satisfied if at least one container exit code
   185→	//   (might be multiple if there are multiple containers not restricted
   186→	//   by the 'containerName' field) is in the set of specified values.
   187→	// - NotIn: the requirement is satisfied if at least one container exit code
   188→	//   (might be multiple if there are multiple containers not restricted
   189→	//   by the 'containerName' field) is not in the set of specified values.
   190→	// Additional values are considered to be added in the future. Clients should
   191→	// react to an unknown operator by assuming the requirement is not satisfied.
   192→	Operator PodFailurePolicyOnExitCodesOperator `json:"operator" protobuf:"bytes,2,req,name=operator"`
   193→
   194→	// Specifies the set of values. Each returned container exit code (might be
   195→	// multiple in case of multiple containers) is checked against this set of
   196→	// values with respect to the operator. The list of values must be ordered
   197→	// and must not contain duplicates. Value '0' cannot be used for the In operator.
   198→	// At least one element is required. At most 255 elements are allowed.
   199→	// +listType=set
   200→	Values []int32 `json:"values" protobuf:"varint,3,rep,name=values"`
   201→}
   202→
   203→// PodFailurePolicyOnPodConditionsPattern describes a pattern for matching
   204→// an actual pod condition type.
   205→type PodFailurePolicyOnPodConditionsPattern struct {
   206→	// Specifies the required Pod condition type. To match a pod condition
   207→	// it is required that specified type equals the pod condition type.
   208→	Type corev1.PodConditionType `json:"type" protobuf:"bytes,1,req,name=type"`
   209→
   210→	// Specifies the required Pod condition status. To match a pod condition
   211→	// it is required that the specified status equals the pod condition status.
   212→	// Defaults to True.
   213→	Status corev1.ConditionStatus `json:"status" protobuf:"bytes,2,req,name=status"`
   214→}
   215→
   216→// PodFailurePolicyRule describes how a pod failure is handled when the requirements are met.
   217→// One of onExitCodes and onPodConditions, but not both, can be used in each rule.
   218→type PodFailurePolicyRule struct {
   219→	// Specifies the action taken on a pod failure when the requirements are satisfied.
   220→	// Possible values are:
   221→	//
   222→	// - FailJob: indicates that the pod's job is marked as Failed and all
   223→	//   running pods are terminated.
   224→	// - FailIndex: indicates that the pod's index is marked as Failed and will
   225→	//   not be restarted.
   226→	//   This value is beta-level. It can be used when the
   227→	//   `JobBackoffLimitPerIndex` feature gate is enabled (enabled by default).
   228→	// - Ignore: indicates that the counter towards the .backoffLimit is not
   229→	//   incremented and a replacement pod is created.
   230→	// - Count: indicates that the pod is handled in the default way - the
   231→	//   counter towards the .backoffLimit is incremented.
   232→	// Additional values are considered to be added in the future. Clients should
   233→	// react to an unknown action by skipping the rule.
   234→	Action PodFailurePolicyAction `json:"action" protobuf:"bytes,1,req,name=action"`
   235→
   236→	// Represents the requirement on the container exit codes.
   237→	// +optional
   238→	OnExitCodes *PodFailurePolicyOnExitCodesRequirement `json:"onExitCodes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,2,opt,name=onExitCodes"`
   239→
   240→	// Represents the requirement on the pod conditions. The requirement is represented
   241→	// as a list of pod condition patterns. The requirement is satisfied if at
   242→	// least one pattern matches an actual pod condition. At most 20 elements are allowed.
   243→	// +listType=atomic
   244→	// +optional
   245→	OnPodConditions []PodFailurePolicyOnPodConditionsPattern `json:"onPodConditions,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,3,opt,name=onPodConditions"`
   246→}
   247→
   248→// PodFailurePolicy describes how failed pods influence the backoffLimit.
   249→type PodFailurePolicy struct {
   250→	// A list of pod failure policy rules. The rules are evaluated in order.
   251→	// Once a rule matches a Pod failure, the remaining of the rules are ignored.
   252→	// When no rule matches the Pod failure, the default handling applies - the
   253→	// counter of pod failures is incremented and it is checked against
   254→	// the backoffLimit. At most 20 elements are allowed.
   255→	// +listType=atomic
   256→	Rules []PodFailurePolicyRule `json:"rules" protobuf:"bytes,1,opt,name=rules"`
   257→}
   258→
   259→// SuccessPolicy describes when a Job can be declared as succeeded based on the success of some indexes.
   260→type SuccessPolicy struct {
   261→	// rules represents the list of alternative rules for the declaring the Jobs
   262→	// as successful before `.status.succeeded >= .spec.completions`. Once any of the rules are met,
   263→	// the "SucceededCriteriaMet" condition is added, and the lingering pods are removed.
   264→	// The terminal state for such a Job has the "Complete" condition.
   265→	// Additionally, these rules are evaluated in order; Once the Job meets one of the rules,
   266→	// other rules are ignored. At most 20 elements are allowed.
   267→	// +listType=atomic
   268→	Rules []SuccessPolicyRule `json:"rules" protobuf:"bytes,1,opt,name=rules"`
   269→}
   270→
   271→// SuccessPolicyRule describes rule for declaring a Job as succeeded.
   272→// Each rule must have at least one of the "succeededIndexes" or "succeededCount" specified.
   273→type SuccessPolicyRule struct {
   274→	// succeededIndexes specifies the set of indexes
   275→	// which need to be contained in the actual set of the succeeded indexes for the Job.
   276→	// The list of indexes must be within 0 to ".spec.completions-1" and
   277→	// must not contain duplicates. At least one element is required.
   278→	// The indexes are represented as intervals separated by commas.
   279→	// The intervals can be a decimal integer or a pair of decimal integers separated by a hyphen.
   280→	// The number are listed in represented by the first and last element of the series,
   281→	// separated by a hyphen.
   282→	// For example, if the completed indexes are 1, 3, 4, 5 and 7, they are
   283→	// represented as "1,3-5,7".
   284→	// When this field is null, this field doesn't default to any value
   285→	// and is never evaluated at any time.
   286→	//
   287→	// +optional
   288→	SucceededIndexes *string `json:"succeededIndexes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,1,opt,name=succeededIndexes"`
   289→
   290→	// succeededCount specifies the minimal required size of the actual set of the succeeded indexes
   291→	// for the Job. When succeededCount is used along with succeededIndexes, the check is
   292→	// constrained only to the set of indexes specified by succeededIndexes.
   293→	// For example, given that succeededIndexes is "1-4", succeededCount is "3",
   294→	// and completed indexes are "1", "3", and "5", the Job isn't declared as succeeded
   295→	// because only "1" and "3" indexes are considered in that rules.
   296→	// When this field is null, this doesn't default to any value and
   297→	// is never evaluated at any time.
   298→	// When specified it needs to be a positive integer.
   299→	//
   300→	// +optional
   301→	SucceededCount *int32 `json:"succeededCount,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,2,opt,name=succeededCount"`
   302→}
   303→
   304→// JobSpec describes how the job execution will look like.
   305→type JobSpec struct {
   306→
   307→	// Specifies the maximum desired number of pods the job should
   308→	// run at any given time. The actual number of pods running in steady state will
   309→	// be less than this number when ((.spec.completions - .status.successful) < .spec.parallelism),
   310→	// i.e. when the work left to do is less than max parallelism.
   311→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/
   312→	// +optional
   313→	Parallelism *int32 `json:"parallelism,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,1,opt,name=parallelism"`
   314→
   315→	// Specifies the desired number of successfully finished pods the
   316→	// job should be run with.  Setting to null means that the success of any
   317→	// pod signals the success of all pods, and allows parallelism to have any positive
   318→	// value.  Setting to 1 means that parallelism is limited to 1 and the success of that
   319→	// pod signals the success of the job.
   320→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/
   321→	// +optional
   322→	Completions *int32 `json:"completions,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,2,opt,name=completions"`
   323→
   324→	// Specifies the duration in seconds relative to the startTime that the job
   325→	// may be continuously active before the system tries to terminate it; value
   326→	// must be positive integer. If a Job is suspended (at creation or through an
   327→	// update), this timer will effectively be stopped and reset when the Job is
   328→	// resumed again.
   329→	// +optional
   330→	ActiveDeadlineSeconds *int64 `json:"activeDeadlineSeconds,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,3,opt,name=activeDeadlineSeconds"`
   331→
   332→	// Specifies the policy of handling failed pods. In particular, it allows to
   333→	// specify the set of actions and conditions which need to be
   334→	// satisfied to take the associated action.
   335→	// If empty, the default behaviour applies - the counter of failed pods,
   336→	// represented by the jobs's .status.failed field, is incremented and it is
   337→	// checked against the backoffLimit. This field cannot be used in combination
   338→	// with restartPolicy=OnFailure.
   339→	//
   340→	// +optional
   341→	PodFailurePolicy *PodFailurePolicy `json:"podFailurePolicy,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,11,opt,name=podFailurePolicy"`
   342→
   343→	// successPolicy specifies the policy when the Job can be declared as succeeded.
   344→	// If empty, the default behavior applies - the Job is declared as succeeded
   345→	// only when the number of succeeded pods equals to the completions.
   346→	// When the field is specified, it must be immutable and works only for the Indexed Jobs.
   347→	// Once the Job meets the SuccessPolicy, the lingering pods are terminated.
   348→	//
   349→	// This field is beta-level. To use this field, you must enable the
   350→	// `JobSuccessPolicy` feature gate (enabled by default).
   351→	// +optional
   352→	SuccessPolicy *SuccessPolicy `json:"successPolicy,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,16,opt,name=successPolicy"`
   353→
   354→	// Specifies the number of retries before marking this job failed.
   355→	// Defaults to 6
   356→	// +optional
   357→	BackoffLimit *int32 `json:"backoffLimit,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,7,opt,name=backoffLimit"`
   358→
   359→	// Specifies the limit for the number of retries within an
   360→	// index before marking this index as failed. When enabled the number of
   361→	// failures per index is kept in the pod's
   362→	// batch.kubernetes.io/job-index-failure-count annotation. It can only
   363→	// be set when Job's completionMode=Indexed, and the Pod's restart
   364→	// policy is Never. The field is immutable.
   365→	// This field is beta-level. It can be used when the `JobBackoffLimitPerIndex`
   366→	// feature gate is enabled (enabled by default).
   367→	// +optional
   368→	BackoffLimitPerIndex *int32 `json:"backoffLimitPerIndex,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,12,opt,name=backoffLimitPerIndex"`
   369→
   370→	// Specifies the maximal number of failed indexes before marking the Job as
   371→	// failed, when backoffLimitPerIndex is set. Once the number of failed
   372→	// indexes exceeds this number the entire Job is marked as Failed and its
   373→	// execution is terminated. When left as null the job continues execution of
   374→	// all of its indexes and is marked with the `Complete` Job condition.
   375→	// It can only be specified when backoffLimitPerIndex is set.
   376→	// It can be null or up to completions. It is required and must be
   377→	// less than or equal to 10^4 when is completions greater than 10^5.
   378→	// This field is beta-level. It can be used when the `JobBackoffLimitPerIndex`
   379→	// feature gate is enabled (enabled by default).
   380→	// +optional
   381→	MaxFailedIndexes *int32 `json:"maxFailedIndexes,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,13,opt,name=maxFailedIndexes"`
   382→
   383→	// TODO enabled it when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/28486 has been fixed
   384→	// Optional number of failed pods to retain.
   385→	// +optional
   386→	// FailedPodsLimit *int32 `json:"failedPodsLimit,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,9,opt,name=failedPodsLimit"`
   387→
   388→	// A label query over pods that should match the pod count.
   389→	// Normally, the system sets this field for you.
   390→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/#label-selectors
   391→	// +optional
   392→	Selector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:"selector,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,4,opt,name=selector"`
   393→
   394→	// manualSelector controls generation of pod labels and pod selectors.
   395→	// Leave `manualSelector` unset unless you are certain what you are doing.
   396→	// When false or unset, the system pick labels unique to this job
   397→	// and appends those labels to the pod template.  When true,
   398→	// the user is responsible for picking unique labels and specifying
   399→	// the selector.  Failure to pick a unique label may cause this
   400→	// and other jobs to not function correctly.  However, You may see
   401→	// `manualSelector=true` in jobs that were created with the old `extensions/v1beta1`
   402→	// API.
   403→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/#specifying-your-own-pod-selector
   404→	// +optional
   405→	ManualSelector *bool `json:"manualSelector,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,5,opt,name=manualSelector"`
   406→
   407→	// Describes the pod that will be created when executing a job.
   408→	// The only allowed template.spec.restartPolicy values are "Never" or "OnFailure".
   409→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/
   410→	Template corev1.PodTemplateSpec `json:"template" protobuf:"bytes,6,opt,name=template"`
   411→
   412→	// ttlSecondsAfterFinished limits the lifetime of a Job that has finished
   413→	// execution (either Complete or Failed). If this field is set,
   414→	// ttlSecondsAfterFinished after the Job finishes, it is eligible to be
   415→	// automatically deleted. When the Job is being deleted, its lifecycle
   416→	// guarantees (e.g. finalizers) will be honored. If this field is unset,
   417→	// the Job won't be automatically deleted. If this field is set to zero,
   418→	// the Job becomes eligible to be deleted immediately after it finishes.
   419→	// +optional
   420→	TTLSecondsAfterFinished *int32 `json:"ttlSecondsAfterFinished,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,8,opt,name=ttlSecondsAfterFinished"`
   421→
   422→	// completionMode specifies how Pod completions are tracked. It can be
   423→	// `NonIndexed` (default) or `Indexed`.
   424→	//
   425→	// `NonIndexed` means that the Job is considered complete when there have
   426→	// been .spec.completions successfully completed Pods. Each Pod completion is
   427→	// homologous to each other.
   428→	//
   429→	// `Indexed` means that the Pods of a
   430→	// Job get an associated completion index from 0 to (.spec.completions - 1),
   431→	// available in the annotation batch.kubernetes.io/job-completion-index.
   432→	// The Job is considered complete when there is one successfully completed Pod
   433→	// for each index.
   434→	// When value is `Indexed`, .spec.completions must be specified and
   435→	// `.spec.parallelism` must be less than or equal to 10^5.
   436→	// In addition, The Pod name takes the form
   437→	// `$(job-name)-$(index)-$(random-string)`,
   438→	// the Pod hostname takes the form `$(job-name)-$(index)`.
   439→	//
   440→	// More completion modes can be added in the future.
   441→	// If the Job controller observes a mode that it doesn't recognize, which
   442→	// is possible during upgrades due to version skew, the controller
   443→	// skips updates for the Job.
   444→	// +optional
   445→	CompletionMode *CompletionMode `json:"completionMode,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,9,opt,name=completionMode,casttype=CompletionMode"`
   446→
   447→	// suspend specifies whether the Job controller should create Pods or not. If
   448→	// a Job is created with suspend set to true, no Pods are created by the Job
   449→	// controller. If a Job is suspended after creation (i.e. the flag goes from
   450→	// false to true), the Job controller will delete all active Pods associated
   451→	// with this Job. Users must design their workload to gracefully handle this.
   452→	// Suspending a Job will reset the StartTime field of the Job, effectively
   453→	// resetting the ActiveDeadlineSeconds timer too. Defaults to false.
   454→	//
   455→	// +optional
   456→	Suspend *bool `json:"suspend,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,10,opt,name=suspend"`
   457→
   458→	// podReplacementPolicy specifies when to create replacement Pods.
   459→	// Possible values are:
   460→	// - TerminatingOrFailed means that we recreate pods
   461→	//   when they are terminating (has a metadata.deletionTimestamp) or failed.
   462→	// - Failed means to wait until a previously created Pod is fully terminated (has phase
   463→	//   Failed or Succeeded) before creating a replacement Pod.
   464→	//
   465→	// When using podFailurePolicy, Failed is the the only allowed value.
   466→	// TerminatingOrFailed and Failed are allowed values when podFailurePolicy is not in use.
   467→	// This is an beta field. To use this, enable the JobPodReplacementPolicy feature toggle.
   468→	// This is on by default.
   469→	// +optional
   470→	PodReplacementPolicy *PodReplacementPolicy `json:"podReplacementPolicy,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,14,opt,name=podReplacementPolicy,casttype=podReplacementPolicy"`
   471→
   472→	// ManagedBy field indicates the controller that manages a Job. The k8s Job
   473→	// controller reconciles jobs which don't have this field at all or the field
   474→	// value is the reserved string `kubernetes.io/job-controller`, but skips
   475→	// reconciling Jobs with a custom value for this field.
   476→	// The value must be a valid domain-prefixed path (e.g. acme.io/foo) -
   477→	// all characters before the first "/" must be a valid subdomain as defined
   478→	// by RFC 1123. All characters trailing the first "/" must be valid HTTP Path
   479→	// characters as defined by RFC 3986. The value cannot exceed 63 characters.
   480→	// This field is immutable.
   481→	//
   482→	// This field is beta-level. The job controller accepts setting the field
   483→	// when the feature gate JobManagedBy is enabled (enabled by default).
   484→	// +optional
   485→	ManagedBy *string `json:"managedBy,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,15,opt,name=managedBy"`
   486→}
   487→
   488→// JobStatus represents the current state of a Job.
   489→type JobStatus struct {
   490→	// The latest available observations of an object's current state. When a Job
   491→	// fails, one of the conditions will have type "Failed" and status true. When
   492→	// a Job is suspended, one of the conditions will have type "Suspended" and
   493→	// status true; when the Job is resumed, the status of this condition will
   494→	// become false. When a Job is completed, one of the conditions will have
   495→	// type "Complete" and status true.
   496→	//
   497→	// A job is considered finished when it is in a terminal condition, either
   498→	// "Complete" or "Failed". A Job cannot have both the "Complete" and "Failed" conditions.
   499→	// Additionally, it cannot be in the "Complete" and "FailureTarget" conditions.
   500→	// The "Complete", "Failed" and "FailureTarget" conditions cannot be disabled.
   501→	//
   502→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/
   503→	// +optional
   504→	// +patchMergeKey=type
   505→	// +patchStrategy=merge
   506→	// +listType=atomic
   507→	Conditions []JobCondition `json:"conditions,omitempty" patchStrategy:"merge" patchMergeKey:"type" protobuf:"bytes,1,rep,name=conditions"`
   508→
   509→	// Represents time when the job controller started processing a job. When a
   510→	// Job is created in the suspended state, this field is not set until the
   511→	// first time it is resumed. This field is reset every time a Job is resumed
   512→	// from suspension. It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.
   513→	//
   514→	// Once set, the field can only be removed when the job is suspended.
   515→	// The field cannot be modified while the job is unsuspended or finished.
   516→	//
   517→	// +optional
   518→	StartTime *metav1.Time `json:"startTime,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,2,opt,name=startTime"`
   519→
   520→	// Represents time when the job was completed. It is not guaranteed to
   521→	// be set in happens-before order across separate operations.
   522→	// It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.
   523→	// The completion time is set when the job finishes successfully, and only then.
   524→	// The value cannot be updated or removed. The value indicates the same or
   525→	// later point in time as the startTime field.
   526→	// +optional
   527→	CompletionTime *metav1.Time `json:"completionTime,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,3,opt,name=completionTime"`
   528→
   529→	// The number of pending and running pods which are not terminating (without
   530→	// a deletionTimestamp).
   531→	// The value is zero for finished jobs.
   532→	// +optional
   533→	Active int32 `json:"active,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,4,opt,name=active"`
   534→
   535→	// The number of pods which reached phase Succeeded.
   536→	// The value increases monotonically for a given spec. However, it may
   537→	// decrease in reaction to scale down of elastic indexed jobs.
   538→	// +optional
   539→	Succeeded int32 `json:"succeeded,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,5,opt,name=succeeded"`
   540→
   541→	// The number of pods which reached phase Failed.
   542→	// The value increases monotonically.
   543→	// +optional
   544→	Failed int32 `json:"failed,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,6,opt,name=failed"`
   545→
   546→	// The number of pods which are terminating (in phase Pending or Running
   547→	// and have a deletionTimestamp).
   548→	//
   549→	// This field is beta-level. The job controller populates the field when
   550→	// the feature gate JobPodReplacementPolicy is enabled (enabled by default).
   551→	// +optional
   552→	Terminating *int32 `json:"terminating,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,11,opt,name=terminating"`
   553→
   554→	// completedIndexes holds the completed indexes when .spec.completionMode =
   555→	// "Indexed" in a text format. The indexes are represented as decimal integers
   556→	// separated by commas. The numbers are listed in increasing order. Three or
   557→	// more consecutive numbers are compressed and represented by the first and
   558→	// last element of the series, separated by a hyphen.
   559→	// For example, if the completed indexes are 1, 3, 4, 5 and 7, they are
   560→	// represented as "1,3-5,7".
   561→	// +optional
   562→	CompletedIndexes string `json:"completedIndexes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,7,opt,name=completedIndexes"`
   563→
   564→	// FailedIndexes holds the failed indexes when spec.backoffLimitPerIndex is set.
   565→	// The indexes are represented in the text format analogous as for the
   566→	// `completedIndexes` field, ie. they are kept as decimal integers
   567→	// separated by commas. The numbers are listed in increasing order. Three or
   568→	// more consecutive numbers are compressed and represented by the first and
   569→	// last element of the series, separated by a hyphen.
   570→	// For example, if the failed indexes are 1, 3, 4, 5 and 7, they are
   571→	// represented as "1,3-5,7".
   572→	// The set of failed indexes cannot overlap with the set of completed indexes.
   573→	//
   574→	// This field is beta-level. It can be used when the `JobBackoffLimitPerIndex`
   575→	// feature gate is enabled (enabled by default).
   576→	// +optional
   577→	FailedIndexes *string `json:"failedIndexes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,10,opt,name=failedIndexes"`
   578→
   579→	// uncountedTerminatedPods holds the UIDs of Pods that have terminated but
   580→	// the job controller hasn't yet accounted for in the status counters.
   581→	//
   582→	// The job controller creates pods with a finalizer. When a pod terminates
   583→	// (succeeded or failed), the controller does three steps to account for it
   584→	// in the job status:
   585→	//
   586→	// 1. Add the pod UID to the arrays in this field.
   587→	// 2. Remove the pod finalizer.
   588→	// 3. Remove the pod UID from the arrays while increasing the corresponding
   589→	//     counter.
   590→	//
   591→	// Old jobs might not be tracked using this field, in which case the field
   592→	// remains null.
   593→	// The structure is empty for finished jobs.
   594→	// +optional
   595→	UncountedTerminatedPods *UncountedTerminatedPods `json:"uncountedTerminatedPods,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,8,opt,name=uncountedTerminatedPods"`
   596→
   597→	// The number of active pods which have a Ready condition and are not
   598→	// terminating (without a deletionTimestamp).
   599→	Ready *int32 `json:"ready,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,9,opt,name=ready"`
   600→}
   601→
   602→// UncountedTerminatedPods holds UIDs of Pods that have terminated but haven't
   603→// been accounted in Job status counters.
   604→type UncountedTerminatedPods struct {
   605→	// succeeded holds UIDs of succeeded Pods.
   606→	// +listType=set
   607→	// +optional
   608→	Succeeded []types.UID `json:"succeeded,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,1,rep,name=succeeded,casttype=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types.UID"`
   609→
   610→	// failed holds UIDs of failed Pods.
   611→	// +listType=set
   612→	// +optional
   613→	Failed []types.UID `json:"failed,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,2,rep,name=failed,casttype=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types.UID"`
   614→}

   616→type JobConditionType string
   617→
   618→// These are built-in conditions of a job.

   740→	// The set of valid time zone names and the time zone offset is loaded from the system-wide time zone
   741→	// database by the API server during CronJob validation and the controller manager during execution.
   742→	// If no system-wide time zone database can be found a bundled version of the database is used instead.

   744→	// configuration, the controller will stop creating new new Jobs and will create a system event with the
   745→	// reason UnknownTimeZone.

    25→const (
    26→	// All Kubernetes labels need to be prefixed with Kubernetes to distinguish them from end-user labels
    27→	// More info: https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#label-selector-and-annotation-conventions
    28→	labelPrefix = "batch.kubernetes.io/"
    29→
    30→	// CronJobScheduledTimestampAnnotation is the scheduled timestamp annotation for the Job.
    31→	// It records the original/expected scheduled timestamp for the running job, represented in RFC3339.
    32→	CronJobScheduledTimestampAnnotation = labelPrefix + "cronjob-scheduled-timestamp"
    33→
    34→	JobCompletionIndexAnnotation = labelPrefix + "job-completion-index"
    35→	// JobTrackingFinalizer is a finalizer for Job's pods. It prevents them from
    36→	// being deleted before being accounted in the Job status.
    37→	//
    38→	// Additionally, the apiserver and job controller use this string as a Job
    39→	// annotation, to mark Jobs that are being tracked using pod finalizers.
    40→	// However, this behavior is deprecated in kubernetes 1.26. This means that, in
    41→	// 1.27+, one release after JobTrackingWithFinalizers graduates to GA, the
    42→	// apiserver and job controller will ignore this annotation and they will
    43→	// always track jobs using finalizers.
    44→	JobTrackingFinalizer = labelPrefix + "job-tracking"
    45→	// The Job labels will use batch.kubernetes.io as a prefix for all labels
    46→	// Historically the job controller uses unprefixed labels for job-name and controller-uid and
    47→	// Kubernetes continutes to recognize those unprefixed labels for consistency.
    48→	JobNameLabel = labelPrefix + "job-name"
    49→	// ControllerUid is used to programatically get pods corresponding to a Job.
    50→	// There is a corresponding label without the batch.kubernetes.io that we support for legacy reasons.
    51→	ControllerUidLabel = labelPrefix + "controller-uid"
    52→	// Annotation indicating the number of failures for the index corresponding
    53→	// to the pod, which are counted towards the backoff limit.
    54→	JobIndexFailureCountAnnotation = labelPrefix + "job-index-failure-count"
    55→	// Annotation indicating the number of failures for the index corresponding
    56→	// to the pod, which don't count towards the backoff limit, according to the
    57→	// pod failure policy. When the annotation is absent zero is implied.
    58→	JobIndexIgnoredFailureCountAnnotation = labelPrefix + "job-index-ignored-failure-count"
    59→	// JobControllerName reserved value for the managedBy field for the built-in
    60→	// Job controller.
    61→	JobControllerName = "kubernetes.io/job-controller"

   102→// CompletionMode specifies how Pod completions of a Job are tracked.
   103→// +enum
   104→type CompletionMode string
   105→
   106→const (
   107→	// NonIndexedCompletion is a Job completion mode. In this mode, the Job is
   108→	// considered complete when there have been .spec.completions
   109→	// successfully completed Pods. Pod completions are homologous to each other.
   110→	NonIndexedCompletion CompletionMode = "NonIndexed"
   111→
   112→	// IndexedCompletion is a Job completion mode. In this mode, the Pods of a
   113→	// Job get an associated completion index from 0 to (.spec.completions - 1).
   114→	// The Job is  considered complete when a Pod completes for each completion
   115→	// index.
   116→	IndexedCompletion CompletionMode = "Indexed"
   117→)

   119→// PodFailurePolicyAction specifies how a Pod failure is handled.
   120→// +enum
   121→type PodFailurePolicyAction string
   122→
   123→const (
   124→	// This is an action which might be taken on a pod failure - mark the
   125→	// pod's job as Failed and terminate all running pods.
   126→	PodFailurePolicyActionFailJob PodFailurePolicyAction = "FailJob"
   127→
   128→	// This is an action which might be taken on a pod failure - mark the
   129→	// Job's index as failed to avoid restarts within this index. This action
   130→	// can only be used when backoffLimitPerIndex is set.
   131→	// This value is beta-level.
   132→	PodFailurePolicyActionFailIndex PodFailurePolicyAction = "FailIndex"
   133→
   134→	// This is an action which might be taken on a pod failure - the counter towards
   135→	// .backoffLimit, represented by the job's .status.failed field, is not
   136→	// incremented and a replacement pod is created.
   137→	PodFailurePolicyActionIgnore PodFailurePolicyAction = "Ignore"
   138→
   139→	// This is an action which might be taken on a pod failure - the pod failure
   140→	// is handled in the default way - the counter towards .backoffLimit,
   141→	// represented by the job's .status.failed field, is incremented.
   142→	PodFailurePolicyActionCount PodFailurePolicyAction = "Count"
   143→)

   145→// +enum

   153→// PodReplacementPolicy specifies the policy for creating pod replacements.

   160→	TerminatingOrFailed PodReplacementPolicy = "TerminatingOrFailed"
   161→	// Failed means to wait until a previously created Pod is fully terminated (has phase
   162→	// Failed or Succeeded) before creating a replacement Pod.

   166→// PodFailurePolicyOnExitCodesRequirement describes the requirement for handling
   167→// a failed pod based on its container exit codes. In particular, it lookups the
   168→// .state.terminated.exitCode for each app container and init container status,
   169→// represented by the .status.containerStatuses and .status.initContainerStatuses
   170→// fields in the Pod status, respectively. Containers completed with success
   171→// (exit code 0) are excluded from the requirement check.
   172→type PodFailurePolicyOnExitCodesRequirement struct {
   173→	// Restricts the check for exit codes to the container with the
   174→	// specified name. When null, the rule applies to all containers.
   175→	// When specified, it should match one the container or initContainer
   176→	// names in the pod template.
   177→	// +optional
   178→	ContainerName *string `json:"containerName,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,1,opt,name=containerName"`
   179→
   180→	// Represents the relationship between the container exit code(s) and the
   181→	// specified values. Containers completed with success (exit code 0) are
   182→	// excluded from the requirement check. Possible values are:
   183→	//
   184→	// - In: the requirement is satisfied if at least one container exit code
   185→	//   (might be multiple if there are multiple containers not restricted
   186→	//   by the 'containerName' field) is in the set of specified values.
   187→	// - NotIn: the requirement is satisfied if at least one container exit code
   188→	//   (might be multiple if there are multiple containers not restricted
   189→	//   by the 'containerName' field) is not in the set of specified values.
   190→	// Additional values are considered to be added in the future. Clients should
   191→	// react to an unknown operator by assuming the requirement is not satisfied.
   192→	Operator PodFailurePolicyOnExitCodesOperator `json:"operator" protobuf:"bytes,2,req,name=operator"`
   193→
   194→	// Specifies the set of values. Each returned container exit code (might be
   195→	// multiple in case of multiple containers) is checked against this set of
   196→	// values with respect to the operator. The list of values must be ordered
   197→	// and must not contain duplicates. Value '0' cannot be used for the In operator.
   198→	// At least one element is required. At most 255 elements are allowed.
   199→	// +listType=set
   200→	Values []int32 `json:"values" protobuf:"varint,3,rep,name=values"`
   201→}

   203→// PodFailurePolicyOnPodConditionsPattern describes a pattern for matching
   204→// an actual pod condition type.

   206→	// Specifies the required Pod condition type. To match a pod condition

   216→// PodFailurePolicyRule describes how a pod failure is handled when the requirements are met.
   217→// One of onExitCodes and onPodConditions, but not both, can be used in each rule.

   223→	//   running pods are terminated.

   225→	//   not be restarted.

   227→	//   `JobBackoffLimitPerIndex` feature gate is enabled (enabled by default).
   228→	// - Ignore: indicates that the counter towards the .backoffLimit is not
   229→	//   incremented and a replacement pod is created.

   239→

   248→// PodFailurePolicy describes how failed pods influence the backoffLimit.
   249→type PodFailurePolicy struct {
   250→	// A list of pod failure policy rules. The rules are evaluated in order.
   251→	// Once a rule matches a Pod failure, the remaining of the rules are ignored.
   252→	// When no rule matches the Pod failure, the default handling applies - the
   253→	// counter of pod failures is incremented and it is checked against

   259→// SuccessPolicy describes when a Job can be declared as succeeded based on the success of some indexes.

   261→	// rules represents the list of alternative rules for the declaring the Jobs
   262→	// as successful before `.status.succeeded >= .spec.completions`. Once any of the rules are met,
   263→	// the "SucceededCriteriaMet" condition is added, and the lingering pods are removed.
   264→	// The terminal state for such a Job has the "Complete" condition.
   265→	// Additionally, these rules are evaluated in order; Once the Job meets one of the rules,
   266→	// other rules are ignored. At most 20 elements are allowed.
   267→	// +listType=atomic
   268→	Rules []SuccessPolicyRule `json:"rules" protobuf:"bytes,1,opt,name=rules"`
   269→}
   270→
   271→// SuccessPolicyRule describes rule for declaring a Job as succeeded.
   272→// Each rule must have at least one of the "succeededIndexes" or "succeededCount" specified.
   273→type SuccessPolicyRule struct {
   274→	// succeededIndexes specifies the set of indexes
   275→	// which need to be contained in the actual set of the succeeded indexes for the Job.
   276→	// The list of indexes must be within 0 to ".spec.completions-1" and
   277→	// must not contain duplicates. At least one element is required.
   278→	// The indexes are represented as intervals separated by commas.
   279→	// The intervals can be a decimal integer or a pair of decimal integers separated by a hyphen.
   280→	// The number are listed in represented by the first and last element of the series,
   281→	// separated by a hyphen.
   282→	// For example, if the completed indexes are 1, 3, 4, 5 and 7, they are
   283→	// represented as "1,3-5,7".
   284→	// When this field is null, this field doesn't default to any value
   285→	// and is never evaluated at any time.
   286→	//
   287→	// +optional
   288→	SucceededIndexes *string `json:"succeededIndexes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,1,opt,name=succeededIndexes"`
   289→
   290→	// succeededCount specifies the minimal required size of the actual set of the succeeded indexes
   291→	// for the Job. When succeededCount is used along with succeededIndexes, the check is
   292→	// constrained only to the set of indexes specified by succeededIndexes.
   293→	// For example, given that succeededIndexes is "1-4", succeededCount is "3",
   294→	// and completed indexes are "1", "3", and "5", the Job isn't declared as succeeded
   295→	// because only "1" and "3" indexes are considered in that rules.
   296→	// When this field is null, this doesn't default to any value and
   297→	// is never evaluated at any time.
   298→	// When specified it needs to be a positive integer.
   299→	//
   300→	// +optional
   301→	SucceededCount *int32 `json:"succeededCount,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,2,opt,name=succeededCount"`
   302→}
   303→
   304→// JobSpec describes how the job execution will look like.
   305→type JobSpec struct {
   306→
   307→	// Specifies the maximum desired number of pods the job should
   308→	// run at any given time. The actual number of pods running in steady state will
   309→	// be less than this number when ((.spec.completions - .status.successful) < .spec.parallelism),
   310→	// i.e. when the work left to do is less than max parallelism.

   312→	// +optional
   313→	Parallelism *int32 `json:"parallelism,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,1,opt,name=parallelism"`
   314→
   315→	// Specifies the desired number of successfully finished pods the
   316→	// job should be run with.  Setting to null means that the success of any
   317→	// pod signals the success of all pods, and allows parallelism to have any positive
   318→	// value.  Setting to 1 means that parallelism is limited to 1 and the success of that
   319→	// pod signals the success of the job.
   320→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/
   321→	// +optional
   322→	Completions *int32 `json:"completions,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,2,opt,name=completions"`
   323→
   324→	// Specifies the duration in seconds relative to the startTime that the job
   325→	// may be continuously active before the system tries to terminate it; value
   326→	// must be positive integer. If a Job is suspended (at creation or through an
   327→	// update), this timer will effectively be stopped and reset when the Job is
   328→	// resumed again.
   329→	// +optional
   330→	ActiveDeadlineSeconds *int64 `json:"activeDeadlineSeconds,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,3,opt,name=activeDeadlineSeconds"`
   331→
   332→	// Specifies the policy of handling failed pods. In particular, it allows to
   333→	// specify the set of actions and conditions which need to be
   334→	// satisfied to take the associated action.
   335→	// If empty, the default behaviour applies - the counter of failed pods,
   336→	// represented by the jobs's .status.failed field, is incremented and it is
   337→	// checked against the backoffLimit. This field cannot be used in combination
   338→	// with restartPolicy=OnFailure.
   339→	//
   340→	// +optional
   341→	PodFailurePolicy *PodFailurePolicy `json:"podFailurePolicy,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,11,opt,name=podFailurePolicy"`
   342→
   343→	// successPolicy specifies the policy when the Job can be declared as succeeded.
   344→	// If empty, the default behavior applies - the Job is declared as succeeded
   345→	// only when the number of succeeded pods equals to the completions.
   346→	// When the field is specified, it must be immutable and works only for the Indexed Jobs.
   347→	// Once the Job meets the SuccessPolicy, the lingering pods are terminated.
   348→	//
   349→	// This field is beta-level. To use this field, you must enable the
   350→	// `JobSuccessPolicy` feature gate (enabled by default).
   351→	// +optional
   352→	SuccessPolicy *SuccessPolicy `json:"successPolicy,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,16,opt,name=successPolicy"`
   353→
   354→	// Specifies the number of retries before marking this job failed.
   355→	// Defaults to 6
   356→	// +optional
   357→	BackoffLimit *int32 `json:"backoffLimit,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,7,opt,name=backoffLimit"`
   358→
   359→	// Specifies the limit for the number of retries within an
   360→	// index before marking this index as failed. When enabled the number of
   361→	// failures per index is kept in the pod's
   362→	// batch.kubernetes.io/job-index-failure-count annotation. It can only
   363→	// be set when Job's completionMode=Indexed, and the Pod's restart
   364→	// policy is Never. The field is immutable.
   365→	// This field is beta-level. It can be used when the `JobBackoffLimitPerIndex`
   366→	// feature gate is enabled (enabled by default).
   367→	// +optional
   368→	BackoffLimitPerIndex *int32 `json:"backoffLimitPerIndex,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,12,opt,name=backoffLimitPerIndex"`
   369→
   370→	// Specifies the maximal number of failed indexes before marking the Job as
   371→	// failed, when backoffLimitPerIndex is set. Once the number of failed
   372→	// indexes exceeds this number the entire Job is marked as Failed and its
   373→	// execution is terminated. When left as null the job continues execution of
   374→	// all of its indexes and is marked with the `Complete` Job condition.
   375→	// It can only be specified when backoffLimitPerIndex is set.
   376→	// It can be null or up to completions. It is required and must be
   377→	// less than or equal to 10^4 when is completions greater than 10^5.
   378→	// This field is beta-level. It can be used when the `JobBackoffLimitPerIndex`
   379→	// feature gate is enabled (enabled by default).
   380→	// +optional
   381→	MaxFailedIndexes *int32 `json:"maxFailedIndexes,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,13,opt,name=maxFailedIndexes"`
   382→
   383→	// TODO enabled it when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/28486 has been fixed
   384→	// Optional number of failed pods to retain.
   385→	// +optional
   386→	// FailedPodsLimit *int32 `json:"failedPodsLimit,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,9,opt,name=failedPodsLimit"`
   387→
   388→	// A label query over pods that should match the pod count.
   389→	// Normally, the system sets this field for you.
   390→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/#label-selectors
   391→	// +optional
   392→	Selector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:"selector,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,4,opt,name=selector"`
   393→
   394→	// manualSelector controls generation of pod labels and pod selectors.
   395→	// Leave `manualSelector` unset unless you are certain what you are doing.
   396→	// When false or unset, the system pick labels unique to this job
   397→	// and appends those labels to the pod template.  When true,
   398→	// the user is responsible for picking unique labels and specifying
   399→	// the selector.  Failure to pick a unique label may cause this
   400→	// and other jobs to not function correctly.  However, You may see
   401→	// `manualSelector=true` in jobs that were created with the old `extensions/v1beta1`
   402→	// API.
   403→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/#specifying-your-own-pod-selector
   404→	// +optional
   405→	ManualSelector *bool `json:"manualSelector,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,5,opt,name=manualSelector"`
   406→
   407→	// Describes the pod that will be created when executing a job.
   408→	// The only allowed template.spec.restartPolicy values are "Never" or "OnFailure".
   409→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/
   410→	Template corev1.PodTemplateSpec `json:"template" protobuf:"bytes,6,opt,name=template"`
   411→
   412→	// ttlSecondsAfterFinished limits the lifetime of a Job that has finished
   413→	// execution (either Complete or Failed). If this field is set,
   414→	// ttlSecondsAfterFinished after the Job finishes, it is eligible to be
   415→	// automatically deleted. When the Job is being deleted, its lifecycle
   416→	// guarantees (e.g. finalizers) will be honored. If this field is unset,
   417→	// the Job won't be automatically deleted. If this field is set to zero,
   418→	// the Job becomes eligible to be deleted immediately after it finishes.
   419→	// +optional
   420→	TTLSecondsAfterFinished *int32 `json:"ttlSecondsAfterFinished,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,8,opt,name=ttlSecondsAfterFinished"`
   421→
   422→	// completionMode specifies how Pod completions are tracked. It can be
   423→	// `NonIndexed` (default) or `Indexed`.
   424→	//
   425→	// `NonIndexed` means that the Job is considered complete when there have
   426→	// been .spec.completions successfully completed Pods. Each Pod completion is
   427→	// homologous to each other.
   428→	//
   429→	// `Indexed` means that the Pods of a
   430→	// Job get an associated completion index from 0 to (.spec.completions - 1),
   431→	// available in the annotation batch.kubernetes.io/job-completion-index.
   432→	// The Job is considered complete when there is one successfully completed Pod
   433→	// for each index.
   434→	// When value is `Indexed`, .spec.completions must be specified and
   435→	// `.spec.parallelism` must be less than or equal to 10^5.
   436→	// In addition, The Pod name takes the form
   437→	// `$(job-name)-$(index)-$(random-string)`,
   438→	// the Pod hostname takes the form `$(job-name)-$(index)`.
   439→	//
   440→	// More completion modes can be added in the future.
   441→	// If the Job controller observes a mode that it doesn't recognize, which
   442→	// is possible during upgrades due to version skew, the controller
   443→	// skips updates for the Job.
   444→	// +optional
   445→	CompletionMode *CompletionMode `json:"completionMode,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,9,opt,name=completionMode,casttype=CompletionMode"`
   446→
   447→	// suspend specifies whether the Job controller should create Pods or not. If
   448→	// a Job is created with suspend set to true, no Pods are created by the Job
   449→	// controller. If a Job is suspended after creation (i.e. the flag goes from
   450→	// false to true), the Job controller will delete all active Pods associated
   451→	// with this Job. Users must design their workload to gracefully handle this.
   452→	// Suspending a Job will reset the StartTime field of the Job, effectively
   453→	// resetting the ActiveDeadlineSeconds timer too. Defaults to false.
   454→	//
   455→	// +optional
   456→	Suspend *bool `json:"suspend,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,10,opt,name=suspend"`
   457→
   458→	// podReplacementPolicy specifies when to create replacement Pods.
   459→	// Possible values are:
   460→	// - TerminatingOrFailed means that we recreate pods
   461→	//   when they are terminating (has a metadata.deletionTimestamp) or failed.
   462→	// - Failed means to wait until a previously created Pod is fully terminated (has phase
   463→	//   Failed or Succeeded) before creating a replacement Pod.
   464→	//
   465→	// When using podFailurePolicy, Failed is the the only allowed value.
   466→	// TerminatingOrFailed and Failed are allowed values when podFailurePolicy is not in use.
   467→	// This is an beta field. To use this, enable the JobPodReplacementPolicy feature toggle.
   468→	// This is on by default.
   469→	// +optional
   470→	PodReplacementPolicy *PodReplacementPolicy `json:"podReplacementPolicy,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,14,opt,name=podReplacementPolicy,casttype=podReplacementPolicy"`
   471→
   472→	// ManagedBy field indicates the controller that manages a Job. The k8s Job
   473→	// controller reconciles jobs which don't have this field at all or the field
   474→	// value is the reserved string `kubernetes.io/job-controller`, but skips
   475→	// reconciling Jobs with a custom value for this field.
   476→	// The value must be a valid domain-prefixed path (e.g. acme.io/foo) -
   477→	// all characters before the first "/" must be a valid subdomain as defined
   478→	// by RFC 1123. All characters trailing the first "/" must be valid HTTP Path
   479→	// characters as defined by RFC 3986. The value cannot exceed 63 characters.
   480→	// This field is immutable.
   481→	//

   483→	// when the feature gate JobManagedBy is enabled (enabled by default).
   484→	// +optional
   485→	ManagedBy *string `json:"managedBy,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,15,opt,name=managedBy"`
   486→}

   488→// JobStatus represents the current state of a Job.
   489→type JobStatus struct {
   490→	// The latest available observations of an object's current state. When a Job
   491→	// fails, one of the conditions will have type "Failed" and status true. When
   492→	// a Job is suspended, one of the conditions will have type "Suspended" and
   493→	// status true; when the Job is resumed, the status of this condition will
   494→	// become false. When a Job is completed, one of the conditions will have
   495→	// type "Complete" and status true.
   496→	//
   497→	// A job is considered finished when it is in a terminal condition, either
   498→	// "Complete" or "Failed". A Job cannot have both the "Complete" and "Failed" conditions.
   499→	// Additionally, it cannot be in the "Complete" and "FailureTarget" conditions.
   500→	// The "Complete", "Failed" and "FailureTarget" conditions cannot be disabled.
   501→	//
   502→	// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/jobs-run-to-completion/
   503→	// +optional
   504→	// +patchMergeKey=type
   505→	// +patchStrategy=merge
   506→	// +listType=atomic
   507→	Conditions []JobCondition `json:"conditions,omitempty" patchStrategy:"merge" patchMergeKey:"type" protobuf:"bytes,1,rep,name=conditions"`
   508→
   509→	// Represents time when the job controller started processing a job. When a
   510→	// Job is created in the suspended state, this field is not set until the
   511→	// first time it is resumed. This field is reset every time a Job is resumed
   512→	// from suspension. It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.
   513→	//
   514→	// Once set, the field can only be removed when the job is suspended.
   515→	// The field cannot be modified while the job is unsuspended or finished.
   516→	//
   517→	// +optional
   518→	StartTime *metav1.Time `json:"startTime,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,2,opt,name=startTime"`
   519→
   520→	// Represents time when the job was completed. It is not guaranteed to
   521→	// be set in happens-before order across separate operations.
   522→	// It is represented in RFC3339 form and is in UTC.
   523→	// The completion time is set when the job finishes successfully, and only then.
   524→	// The value cannot be updated or removed. The value indicates the same or
   525→	// later point in time as the startTime field.
   526→	// +optional
   527→	CompletionTime *metav1.Time `json:"completionTime,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,3,opt,name=completionTime"`
   528→
   529→	// The number of pending and running pods which are not terminating (without
   530→	// a deletionTimestamp).
   531→	// The value is zero for finished jobs.
   532→	// +optional
   533→	Active int32 `json:"active,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,4,opt,name=active"`
   534→
   535→	// The number of pods which reached phase Succeeded.
   536→	// The value increases monotonically for a given spec. However, it may
   537→	// decrease in reaction to scale down of elastic indexed jobs.
   538→	// +optional
   539→	Succeeded int32 `json:"succeeded,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,5,opt,name=succeeded"`
   540→
   541→	// The number of pods which reached phase Failed.
   542→	// The value increases monotonically.
   543→	// +optional
   544→	Failed int32 `json:"failed,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,6,opt,name=failed"`
   545→
   546→	// The number of pods which are terminating (in phase Pending or Running
   547→	// and have a deletionTimestamp).
   548→	//
   549→	// This field is beta-level. The job controller populates the field when
   550→	// the feature gate JobPodReplacementPolicy is enabled (enabled by default).
   551→	// +optional
   552→	Terminating *int32 `json:"terminating,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,11,opt,name=terminating"`
   553→
   554→	// completedIndexes holds the completed indexes when .spec.completionMode =
   555→	// "Indexed" in a text format. The indexes are represented as decimal integers
   556→	// separated by commas. The numbers are listed in increasing order. Three or
   557→	// more consecutive numbers are compressed and represented by the first and
   558→	// last element of the series, separated by a hyphen.
   559→	// For example, if the completed indexes are 1, 3, 4, 5 and 7, they are
   560→	// represented as "1,3-5,7".
   561→	// +optional
   562→	CompletedIndexes string `json:"completedIndexes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,7,opt,name=completedIndexes"`
   563→
   564→	// FailedIndexes holds the failed indexes when spec.backoffLimitPerIndex is set.
   565→	// The indexes are represented in the text format analogous as for the
   566→	// `completedIndexes` field, ie. they are kept as decimal integers
   567→	// separated by commas. The numbers are listed in increasing order. Three or
   568→	// more consecutive numbers are compressed and represented by the first and
   569→	// last element of the series, separated by a hyphen.
   570→	// For example, if the failed indexes are 1, 3, 4, 5 and 7, they are
   571→	// represented as "1,3-5,7".
   572→	// The set of failed indexes cannot overlap with the set of completed indexes.
   573→	//
   574→	// This field is beta-level. It can be used when the `JobBackoffLimitPerIndex`
   575→	// feature gate is enabled (enabled by default).
   576→	// +optional
   577→	FailedIndexes *string `json:"failedIndexes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,10,opt,name=failedIndexes"`
   578→
   579→	// uncountedTerminatedPods holds the UIDs of Pods that have terminated but
   580→	// the job controller hasn't yet accounted for in the status counters.
   581→	//
   582→	// The job controller creates pods with a finalizer. When a pod terminates
   583→	// (succeeded or failed), the controller does three steps to account for it
   584→	// in the job status:
   585→	//
   586→	// 1. Add the pod UID to the arrays in this field.
   587→	// 2. Remove the pod finalizer.
   588→	// 3. Remove the pod UID from the arrays while increasing the corresponding
   589→	//     counter.
   590→	//
   591→	// Old jobs might not be tracked using this field, in which case the field
   592→	// remains null.
   593→	// The structure is empty for finished jobs.
   594→	// +optional
   595→	UncountedTerminatedPods *UncountedTerminatedPods `json:"uncountedTerminatedPods,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,8,opt,name=uncountedTerminatedPods"`
   596→
   597→	// The number of active pods which have a Ready condition and are not
   598→	// terminating (without a deletionTimestamp).
   599→	Ready *int32 `json:"ready,omitempty" protobuf:"varint,9,opt,name=ready"`
   600→}
   601→
   602→// UncountedTerminatedPods holds UIDs of Pods that have terminated but haven't
   603→// been accounted in Job status counters.
   604→type UncountedTerminatedPods struct {
   605→	// succeeded holds UIDs of succeeded Pods.
   606→	// +listType=set
   607→	// +optional
   608→	Succeeded []types.UID `json:"succeeded,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,1,rep,name=succeeded,casttype=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types.UID"`
   609→
   610→	// failed holds UIDs of failed Pods.
   611→	// +listType=set
   612→	// +optional
   613→	Failed []types.UID `json:"failed,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,2,rep,name=failed,casttype=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types.UID"`
   614→}

   616→type JobConditionType string
   617→
   618→// These are built-in conditions of a job.
   619→const (
   620→	// JobSuspended means the job has been suspended.
   621→	JobSuspended JobConditionType = "Suspended"
   622→	// JobComplete means the job has completed its execution.
   623→	JobComplete JobConditionType = "Complete"
   624→	// JobFailed means the job has failed its execution.
   625→	JobFailed JobConditionType = "Failed"
   626→	// FailureTarget means the job is about to fail its execution.
   627→	JobFailureTarget JobConditionType = "FailureTarget"
   628→	// JobSuccessCriteriaMet means the Job has been succeeded.
   629→	JobSuccessCriteriaMet JobConditionType = "SuccessCriteriaMet"
   630→)
   631→
   632→const (
   633→	// JobReasonPodFailurePolicy reason indicates a job failure condition is added due to
   634→	// a failed pod matching a pod failure policy rule
   635→	// https://kep.k8s.io/3329
   636→	JobReasonPodFailurePolicy string = "PodFailurePolicy"
   637→	// JobReasonBackOffLimitExceeded reason indicates that pods within a job have failed a number of
   638→	// times higher than backOffLimit times.
   639→	JobReasonBackoffLimitExceeded string = "BackoffLimitExceeded"
   640→	// JobReasponDeadlineExceeded means job duration is past ActiveDeadline
   641→	JobReasonDeadlineExceeded string = "DeadlineExceeded"
   642→	// JobReasonMaxFailedIndexesExceeded indicates that an indexed of a job failed
   643→	// This const is used in beta-level feature: https://kep.k8s.io/3850.
   644→	JobReasonMaxFailedIndexesExceeded string = "MaxFailedIndexesExceeded"
   645→	// JobReasonFailedIndexes means Job has failed indexes.
   646→	// This const is used in beta-level feature: https://kep.k8s.io/3850.
   647→	JobReasonFailedIndexes string = "FailedIndexes"
   648→	// JobReasonSuccessPolicy reason indicates a SuccessCriteriaMet condition is added due to
   649→	// a Job met successPolicy.
   650→	// https://kep.k8s.io/3998
   651→	// This is currently a beta field.
   652→	JobReasonSuccessPolicy string = "SuccessPolicy"
   653→	// JobReasonCompletionsReached reason indicates a SuccessCriteriaMet condition is added due to
   654→	// a number of succeeded Job pods met completions.
   655→	// - https://kep.k8s.io/3998
   656→	// This is currently a beta field.
   657→	JobReasonCompletionsReached string = "CompletionsReached"
   658→)

   697→// CronJob represents the configuration of a single cron job.
